topic: AI for Robotics Process Automation (RPA) and Physical Automation in 2026

competitors.title: UiPath vs Automation Anywhere vs Power Automate: Enterprise RPA Compared (April 2026)
key_points: UiPath is the enterprise RPA leader with mature orchestration, largest bot marketplace, and deep AI integration
Automation Anywhere is cloud-native challenger emphasizing process discovery and generative AI
Power Automate is Microsoft's ecosystem play, costing 60-75% less but with limitations on complex processes
UiPath AI Center provides pre-trained ML models for document understanding, sentiment analysis, text classification
Automation Anywhere IQ Bot uses computer vision and NLP for document extraction with learning loop
Power Automate AI Builder provides pre-built models but struggles with variable-layout documents
UiPath supports on-premises, cloud, and hybrid deployments; Automation Anywhere is cloud-native by design
Pricing: Power Automate $15/user/month attended, $150/bot/month unattended; UiPath Pro $420-$1,380 per user/year; Automation Anywhere custom quote

competitors.title: AI Agents vs RPA in 2026: What Actually Automates Work and What Breaks in Production
key_points: RPA is deterministic automation: great for repetitive, stable, rule-based workflows with structured inputs
AI agents are probabilistic automation: handle unstructured inputs, messy workflows, reasoning, decision-making
RPA fails when UI changes, fields move, or exceptions occur frequently
AI agents fail when workflow requires zero mistakes, tool permissions too broad, or lack of observability
Hybrid architecture: agents for cognition (understanding intent, extracting fields, handling exceptions), RPA for execution (deterministic clicks, compliance-sensitive tasks)
Validation before execution: check required fields, value formats, confidence thresholds, permission scope
Gating required for irreversible actions: human approval, policy approval, staged execution

competitors.title: Agentic AI vs RPA: The Enterprise ROI Comparison Every CTO Needs in 2026
key_points: Agentic AI delivers 2–3× more net value than RPA over 3-year TCO horizon for complex, judgment-intensive workflows
RPA achieves ROI in 6–18 months for deterministic processes but licensing/maintenance costs compound after year 1
Teams deploying Agentic AI report 67% faster deployment cycles and 71% infrastructure cost reduction on Kubernetes
Fortune 500 migration pattern: RPA for deterministic 70%, Agentic AI for exception-heavy 30%, cutting manual intervention 50–70%
McKinsey estimates 60–70% of enterprise workflows contain judgment-intensive steps RPA cannot handle
Agentic AI adoption in Fortune 500 accelerated 340% in 2025
RPA maintenance tax: bots break with UI changes, screen layout shifts, requiring 'bot janitors'
